raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The budget approved by Governor Newsom allocates $82.5 million toward reducing health care deductibles and co-pays for Covered California participants.",
    "A survey conducted by the California Health Care Foundation in 2021 found that nearly half of respondents cited cost as a reason for skipping care in the prior year.",
    "A quarter of survey respondents experienced or knew a family member who had difficulty paying a medical bill.",
    "The percentage of low-income Californians who faced difficulty paying a medical bill jumps to forty-three percent.",
    "$82.5 million would benefit an estimated 650,000 current Covered California enrollees.",
    "$165 million is allocated for 2025 and beyond to directly reduce out-of-pocket costs for low-income Californians.",
    "The federal Inflation Reduction Act caps rate increases at 8.5% of income."
  ]
}
```
